channel.title: DJ's Website – Models
channel.description: Recent content in Models on DJ's Website

item.title: Docs: Selection
item.description:
Ill-posed Problem

  • The data itself is not sufficient to find a unique solution

Induction Bias

  • The set of assumptions we make to have learning possible
  • Leaning is not possible iwhout inductive bias

Model Selection

  • About choosing the right bias

Model Generalization

  • How well a model trained on the training set predicts the right output for new instances
  • Underfitting
  • Overfitting

Triple Trade-Off

  • The complexity of the hypothesis we fit to data
  • The amount of training data
  • The generalization error on new examples

Cross Validation

  • Chose the hypotheis that is best on the validation set

Notes

  • Version spaces
  • Near miss
    • proposed by Winston 1975
    • a negative example that is very much like a positive example
  • Active learning:
    • proposed by Angluin 1988
    • an algorithm that can generate instances itself and ask for them tobe labeled
    • instad of passively being given them
  • VC dimension
    • proposed by Vapnik and Chervonenkis in the early 1970s
    • Nothing is more than a good theory
  • PAC model
    • proposed by Valient (1984)

Ref

  • Ethem Alpaydin, 2020, Introduction to Machine Learning
